SEARCH
Package io.github.lucfr1746.llibrary.itemstack

Record Class BookBuilder.WrittenBookBuilder

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Record
io.github.lucfr1746.llibrary.itemstack.BookBuilder.WrittenBookBuilder
Record Components:
writtenBook - the written book ItemStack. Must not be null and must have BookMeta.
Enclosing class:
BookBuilder
public static record BookBuilder.WrittenBookBuilder(@NotNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack writtenBook) extends Record
A builder class for creating and modifying written books in Minecraft.

This builder provides methods to manage metadata such as title, author, and generation for a written book ItemStack. The input ItemStack is cloned to maintain immutability.

  • Constructor Summary

    WrittenBookBuilder(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack writtenBook)
    Constructs a new WrittenBookBuilder from the specified written book ItemStack.

  • Constructor Details

    • WrittenBookBuilder

      public WrittenBookBuilder(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack writtenBook)
      Constructs a new WrittenBookBuilder from the specified written book ItemStack.

      The provided ItemStack must be a valid written book with BookMeta. The original item is cloned to maintain immutability.

      Parameters:
      writtenBook - the written book ItemStack. Must not be null and must have BookMeta.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if writtenBook is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if writtenBook is Material.AIR.
      IllegalStateException - if writtenBook does not have BookMeta.

  • Method Details

    • build

      public org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack build()
      Builds and returns a new ItemStack based on the current state of this WrittenBookBuilder.
      Returns:
      a new cloned ItemStack representing the written book.

    • getAuthor

      public String getAuthor()
      Retrieves the author of the written book.
      Returns:
      the author's name, or null if not set.

    • getGeneration

      public org.bukkit.inventory.meta.BookMeta.Generation getGeneration()
      Retrieves the generation of the written book.
      Returns:
      the BookMeta.Generation of the book, or null if not set.

    • getTitle

      public String getTitle()
      Retrieves the title of the written book.
      Returns:
      the title of the book, or null if not set.

    • hasAuthor

      public boolean hasAuthor()
      Checks whether the book has an author set.
      Returns:
      true if the book has an author, otherwise false.

    • hasGeneration

      public boolean hasGeneration()
      Checks whether the book has a generation set.
      Returns:
      true if the book has a generation, otherwise false.

    • hasTitle

      public boolean hasTitle()
      Checks whether the book has a title set.
      Returns:
      true if the book has a title, otherwise false.

      Sets the author of the written book.
      Parameters:

      public BookBuilder.WrittenBookBuilder setGeneration(org.bukkit.inventory.meta.BookMeta.Generation generation)

      Returns:

    • setTitle

      public BookBuilder.WrittenBookBuilder setTitle(String title)
      Sets the title of the written book.

      Returns:
      the current WrittenBookBuilder instance.

    • toItemBuilder

      public ItemBuilder toItemBuilder()
      Converts the current WrittenBookBuilder to an ItemBuilder for further manipulation.
      Returns:
      a new ItemBuilder initialized with the current written book.

    • toString

      public final String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this record class. The representation contains the name of the class, followed by the name and value of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      toString in class Record
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object

    • hashCode

      public final int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this object. The value is derived from the hash code of each of the record components.
      Specified by:
      hashCode in class Record
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • equals

      public final boolean equals(Object o)
      Indicates whether some other object is "equal to" this one. The objects are equal if the other object is of the same class and if all the record components are equal. All components in this record class are compared with Objects::equals(Object,Object).
      Specified by:
      equals in class Record
      Parameters:
      o - the object with which to compare
      Returns:
      true if this object is the same as the o argument; false otherwise.
